title: CSD Autocorrelation Precursor to State Transitions
rejected_hypothesis: Pre-transition weeks into S0 exhibit statistically significant spikes in rolling lag-1 autocorrelation.
empirical_evidence: t-statistic of 1.01 (p-value = 0.32) fails to reject the null hypothesis of equal autocorrelation between pre-transition and normal weeks.
impact_on_architecture: CSD autocorrelation cannot be used as an early-warning transition trigger.

title: Memoryless First-Order Markov Residence
rejected_hypothesis: State residence durations follow a memoryless Exponential distribution with constant hazard rates.
empirical_evidence: Log-Normal distribution minimizes AIC across all 5 states (e.g. S2 Log-Normal AIC 131.7 vs Exponential 145.0), proving non-Markovian duration dependence.
impact_on_architecture: Replaced pure Markov transition matrix with semi-Markov Log-Normal duration models.

title: S0 Binary Transition Prediction via Lagged Linear Features
rejected_hypothesis: Logistic regression trained on lagged geometry features can forecast binary transitions into S0 out-of-sample.
empirical_evidence: Validation Brier Skill Score was -2.44 and Frozen Test BSS was -2.55 (worse than climatological probability). Precision and Recall were 0.0% at optimal decision thresholds.
impact_on_architecture: Abandoned hard binary state switches in favor of continuous coordinate risk scaling.

title: Out-of-Sample S0 Alpha Attribution & Occurrence
rejected_hypothesis: S0 state alpha outperformance is externally validated out-of-sample across 2023-2026.
empirical_evidence: Strict chronological auditing proves zero S0 weeks occurred out-of-sample (0 / 170 weeks in 2023-2026 Validation & Frozen Test). S0 outperformance is an In-Sample (2019-2022) discovery artifact.
impact_on_architecture: Removed S0 out-of-sample alpha claims; state labels are designated strictly as analytical interpretations rather than live control inputs.

title: ElasticNet Incremental Forecasting Skill for Effective Breadth
rejected_hypothesis: ElasticNet multivariate lagged regression provides incremental forecasting value for effective breadth beyond simple autocorrelation.
empirical_evidence: Naive AR(1) persistence (pred(t+1) = B(t)) achieves out-of-sample R2 of 0.8798 (Validation) and 0.9290 (Frozen Test), outperforming ElasticNet (0.8527 and 0.9047). High R2 is entirely driven by slow-moving autocorrelation.
impact_on_architecture: De-emphasized ElasticNet feature skill; effective breadth is recognized as a persistent AR(1) coordinate.

title: Dynamic Kappa Drawdown Control Dominance over Constant Leverage
rejected_hypothesis: Dynamic forecast-based kappa scaling provides superior drawdown control compared to a constant-exposure benchmark at the same average leverage.
empirical_evidence: Constant exposure (kappa_bar = 0.76/0.83) achieves smaller Max Drawdowns in both windows (-8.73% Val, -10.84% Test) than Dynamic kappa (-9.12% Val, -11.80% Test). Paired t-tests show return differences are statistically insignificant (p = 0.25 Val, p = 0.94 Test).
impact_on_architecture: Rewrote Section 8.2/9.2 to explicitly state Dynamic kappa functions primarily as average de-leveraging rather than an information-driven risk valve.
